key_insight: Cr alloying in RuO2 does not induce the proposed altermagnetic state; instead, the material shows competing ferromagnetic and antiferromagnetic behavior consistent with localized holes on Cr ions.
engineering_relevance: This work clarifies that simple hole-doping strategies may fail to tune RuO2 into altermagnetic phases, informing thin-film design and magnetic materials engineering for spintronic applications.
method: Reactive magnetron co-sputtering of Ru1−xCrxO2 thin films on TiO2 substrates
X-ray diffraction and XPS depth profiling with Ar etching to verify epitaxy and composition
Low-temperature neutron diffraction and temperature-dependent magnetometry to probe magnetic order
evidence_signal: Neutron diffraction found no long-range altermagnetic order for x = 0 and 0.23, while susceptibility and hysteresis measurements showed antiferromagnetic signatures coexisting with ferromagnetism at higher Cr content.
watch_item: The magnetic response appears clustered and mixed, so interpreting the signal as intrinsic bulk altermagnetism would be misleading without further phase-separation analysis.
paper_title: Competing ferromagnetic and antiferromagnetic interactions in non-altermagnetic Ru$_{1-x}$Cr$_{x}$O$_{2}$
